title: Project Doctor and Profile
outOfScope: Automatic approval of Project Profile boundaries, Guards, quality commands, or Adoption Readiness
Product source code, deployment configuration, and external production operations
Claims that Doctor/Profile evidence is a security, compliance, sandbox, identity, or tamper-proof audit guarantee

sources.reason: Serial remediation plan and WI06 acceptance target.
Repository Governance Layer, Profile, evidence, and boundary terminology.
Existing read-only project fact scanner and proposal inputs.
Confirmed Profile schema and approval validation.
Proposal generation and explicit confirmation boundary.
Existing stack detection, Profile, and calibration behavior tests.
Adoption Configure workflow and human confirmation boundary.
Japanese adoption Configure workflow and human confirmation boundary.

riskAssessment.reason: Project facts and boundary proposals can be incomplete or ambiguous; no proposal may be treated as approval.

executionDecision.reason: User authorized the serial remediation plan and complete Work Item lifecycle.

preReviewWarnings: Doctor remains read-only; calibration writes only a new proposal; critical-domain and unknown findings stay visible until explicit human confirmation.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Project Doctor detects Python, Flutter/Dart, TypeScript/JavaScript, Java/Kotlin, and Go signals with evidence and confidence, without modifying project files.

Doctor and calibration produce a reviewable Profile Proposal containing detected facts, candidate module boundaries, quality-command candidates, critical-domain signals, and unresolved unknowns; proposals are never treated as confirmed configuration.
Confirmed Profile validation requires explicit approval metadata and blocks unresolved blocking unknowns; calibration does not overwrite Guards or an existing proposal.
Focused tests cover positive stack detection, ambiguous or missing evidence, critical-domain/unknown visibility, read-only Doctor behavior, and explicit Profile approval.
English and Japanese adoption documentation describes Doctor, proposal review, confirmation, and the boundary that project-specific quality commands remain unconfigured until confirmation.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: The Configure Work Item needs an evidence-backed, project-calibrated view of languages, modules, quality commands, and critical domains before a project Profile can be confirmed. Existing detection and proposal paths must make ambiguity and unknowns visible without silently approving boundaries or mutating project-owned configuration.
constraints: Doctor is read-only except for its explicitly requested report under target/.
Calibration may create a new project_profile.proposed.yaml only when the target does not already exist; it must not overwrite Guards or confirmed project configuration.
Detected, proposed, required user input, and confirmed values remain distinct; missing or ambiguous evidence is not equivalent to Allow or Ready.
Use standard-library implementation and preserve template-maintenance behavior, existing Profile schema, and explicit human approval semantics.
Do not modify product source code or claim security, compliance, sandbox, or production-readiness guarantees.
intent.rationale: A conservative Doctor/Profile proposal gives the adopter a concrete review surface while preserving human ownership of project boundaries and quality policy.

rawUserRequest: 先走完当前文档的流程。用户已授权按计划逐工单执行，包含修改代码、测试、文档、PR、合并、ai-close-work-item、分支清理；妄想测试失败继续修改。

declaredIntent.summary: Detect project stacks and boundaries and generate a conservative Project Profile proposal for explicit human confirmation.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: supported stack signals produce evidence and confidence
missing or ambiguous project evidence remains visible as unknown and does not auto-approve
Doctor and calibration preserve read-only and no-overwrite boundaries
confirmed Profile requires explicit approval and resolved blocking unknowns

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized governance code, tests, documentation, PR, merge, archive, close, and branch cleanup.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
